hero.label: Hero
hero.heading: A new kind of memory care, coming to East Texas.
hero.body: The Memory Health Life Center will give 54 East Texans living with dementia a place to live with dignity, purpose, and daily life, and train the caregivers our region needs.

vision.label: The Vision
vision.heading: A dementia care campus built around daily life.
vision.body: Inspired by the Hogeweyk model from the Netherlands and adapted for East Texas families, the Memory Health Life Center is being designed as a residential community, not a hallway. Residents will live in homes, walk the grounds, tend gardens and animals, and share daily life with neighbors, caregivers, and visiting students.

need.label: Why Memory Care Matters
need.heading: Rural East Texas families are facing dementia alone.
need.body: The need is here. The current options aren't enough. Families travel for hours to reach specialists, caregivers carry years of around-the-clock care without support, and existing facilities too often default to confinement.

services.label: What the Center Will Provide
services.heading: Care that looks like a life, not a hallway.
services.body: Homes, a working farm, gardens, classrooms, and the people to make it real, under one roof, in one community.

partners.label: A Regional Resource
partners.heading: Built in Quitman. Built for the region.
partners.body: Adjacent to UT Health Quitman Hospital. A teaching site for the University of Texas at Tyler School of Medicine. Designed to serve East Texas families, including low- and moderate-income families, with a model that prizes daily life over containment.

support.label: Support the Campaign
support.heading: Help build it.
support.body: A $60M+ capital project, made possible one gift at a time. Federal funds are committed. Community gifts are growing. Every dollar moves the Memory Health Life Center closer to opening day.

progress.label: Project Progress
progress.heading: Where we are right now.
progress.body: From a 2020 kitchen-table conversation to a federally backed regional project, the Memory Health Life Center is being built in plain view.

families.label: Families & Caregivers
families.heading: If you're caring for someone with dementia, we see you.
families.body: The Memory Health Life Center is being built for families like yours. While we're building, we're here with resources, events, and a place to stay informed.

updates.label: Latest Updates
updates.heading: News from the Memory Health Life Center
updates.body: In-depth coverage of the Memory Health Life Center vision and the path to state matching funds.

final_cta.label: Final Donate / Contact
final_cta.heading: Every gift, large or small, moves this closer to opening day.
final_cta.body: Make a gift online, or talk with our team about major gifts, naming opportunities, planned giving, and partnership.

hero.label: Hero
hero.heading: A campus for living and learning.
hero.body: The Memory Health Life Center is a 54-bed, 29-acre brain-health village rising in Quitman, Texas, adjacent to UT Health Quitman. This page walks through the mission, the site, the care model, the numbers, and the downloads behind it.

mission.label: The Mission
mission.heading: What we are here to do.
mission.body: The Wood County Health Care Foundation began with one promise: build a place where families do not have to leave East Texas to find good dementia care, and where the people who provide that care can learn how to do it well.

site.label: The Site
site.heading: 29 acres, next door to a hospital.
site.body: The Center for Memory Health & Education rises on 29 acres owned by the Wood County Central Hospital District, directly adjacent to UT Health's Quitman Hospital campus. The siting is intentional — a clinical neighbor a short walk away, and a regional position that puts millions of Texans within reach.

campus.label: The Campus
campus.heading: Six places, one campus.
campus.body: Not a single building with corridors and doors. A village — residential dwellings, a clinic, a barn, gardens, reflection grounds, and student housing — designed so daily life feels like a neighborhood, not an institution.

care_model.label: Care Model
care_model.heading: Hogeweyk-inspired. Zeisel-trained.
care_model.body: The care model was pioneered by John Zeisel, PhD — a leading expert on dementia care and a former Harvard professor — and built on five foundational principles. The same five principles shape every program, every space, and every interaction on the campus.

downloads.label: Downloads
downloads.heading: Take the deck with you.
downloads.body: For donors, board members, partners, and journalists who need the full picture — the foundation maintains a current presentation deck and the most recent conceptual drawings of the campus.

hero.label: Hero
hero.heading: The Wood County Health Care Foundation.
hero.body: A 501(c)(3) nonprofit stewarding health care in Wood County since 1989. Today, leading the work to build the Memory Health Life Center in Quitman, Texas.
hero.cta_label: Donate to the Memory Health Life Center

charter.label: Charter
charter.heading: Our purpose.
charter.body: Drawn verbatim from the Foundation's Restated Certificate of Formation, approved by the Texas Secretary of State on September 2, 2022.

history.label: History
history.heading: From 1989 to today.
history.body: Four moments in the Foundation's story so far.

board.label: Board & Leadership
board.heading: Members.
board.body: Voting board members from across Wood County, East Texas, and the broader health care, business, and civic community.

hero.label: Hero
hero.heading: Build it with us.
hero.body: A $60M+ capital project, made possible one gift at a time. Federal funds are committed. Community gifts are growing. Every gift moves the Memory Health Life Center closer to opening day.

donate_form.label: Donate Form Intro
donate_form.heading: Make a secure gift.
donate_form.body: Choose your amount, add an optional dedication, and continue to secure payment. Gifts are tax-deductible to the extent allowed by law.

other_ways.label: Other Ways to Give
other_ways.heading: Many paths. One purpose.
other_ways.body: Some of the most meaningful gifts arrive by check, by bequest, or through a corporate match. We will walk every donor through the path that fits their plan.

hero.label: Hero
hero.heading: What's coming up.
hero.body: Caregiver workshops, community gatherings, foundation briefings, and fundraising events from the Memory Health Life Center and the Wood County Health Care Foundation.

upcoming.label: Upcoming Events
upcoming.heading: Pick the kind of gathering for you.
upcoming.body: A morning of practical training on communication, daily care, and self-care for family caregivers of loved ones with dementia. Coffee provided.

notify.label: Event Notifications
notify.heading: Get event reminders by email.
notify.body: One email when something is scheduled. No spam, no fundraising appeals from this list — just the next caregiver workshop, the next site tour, the next foundation briefing.

hero.label: Hero
hero.heading: Built, gift by gift.
hero.body: The families, individuals, and organizations whose founding contributions made the Memory Health Life Center possible. We are honored to recognize each of them.

founders.label: Founders Roll
founders.heading: Organizations & individuals — $10,000 or more.
founders.body: These founding contributors stepped forward at the very beginning. Their leadership unlocked the federal earmark, attracted regional partners, and set the campaign in motion.

hero.label: Hero
hero.heading: On the record.
hero.body: Federal, state, academic, medical, and community leaders have put their names behind the Memory Health Life Center. The letters below are excerpted with attribution; full signed letters are on file with the Wood County Health Care Foundation.

hero.label: Hero
hero.heading: Trusted help, when you need it.
hero.body: A curated set of resources for dementia caregivers and East Texas families: 24/7 helplines, local support, national organizations, and education. We've vetted every link so you don't have to.

crisis.label: Crisis Helpline
crisis.heading: Need to talk to someone right now?
crisis.body: The Alzheimer's Association 24/7 Helpline is staffed day and night by trained, master's-level clinicians. They handle crisis calls, referrals to local community programs, dementia-related education, and emotional support, in your preferred language through their interpreter service.

hero.label: Hero
hero.heading: Get in touch.
hero.body: Fill out the form below and a member of the Wood County Health Care Foundation team will be in touch within one business day. For urgent caregiver questions, the Alzheimer's Association 24/7 Helpline is on the Resources page.

form_intro.label: Form Introduction
form_intro.heading: We read every message.
form_intro.body: Tell us what you're reaching out about and the best way to get back to you. A foundation team member will respond within one business day.
